identifier: Communications of the ACM, vol. 13, no. 7, pp. 422-426; doi:10.1145/362686.362692
summary: Introduces the Bloom filter: hash-coded set membership that permits a controlled rate of false positives in exchange for dramatic space savings, with no false negatives. Derives the space/error trade-off analysis from which the standard false-positive formula and the optimal number of hash functions follow.
title: Space/Time Trade-offs in Hash Coding with Allowable Errors
